free_text: The 16O(d,n)17F reaction at deuteron energies from 2.5
to 5.5 MeV.
5.5 MeV
A NE 102 A plastic scintillator 10.2 cm in
diameter and 5.1 cm thick coupled to an RCA 7264
photomultiplier. The flight path was maintained at
3.00 m throughout the experiment.
As oxygen target a gas cell (purity 99.5%) consisting o
an internally gold-plated brass cylinder was used.The
entrance window of the target cell was a 2.2 mg/cm2 Ni
foil, silver soldered to the end of the inner tube of
the target.
The experimental data were corrected for the relative
detector efficiency.
Total error.The uncertainty of the absolute
normalization has many components and these were
estimated as follows:+-2% in the number of incident
deuterons,+-3% in the target thickness (composed of 1%
temperature,+-1% in gas pressure,+-0.25% in gas purity
and +-2.5% in target length).The uncertainty in the
proton recoil counter measurements was +-10% where the
largest contribution (+-9%) is related to the
determination of the geometrical factor.The expression
for the proton recoil counter efficiency also contains
n-p cross section which has been reported to be
determined with a precision of +-2% or better.The
accuracy of the hydrogen content of the polythene foil
was taken to be within 2% and the statistical
uncertainty of the measurement was 3%.
The statistical errors in the all measurements were
less than 1%.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Relative error
The uncertainty of the incident deuteron energy was
estimated to be +-25 keV around 3 MeV and +-20 keV
around 5 MeV.
Angle is given in lab. system.
